title: Abdominal Pain - Functional Constipation
chief_complaint: 5-year-old with abdominal pain and straining with bowel movements for 2 months. Bowel movements every 4-5 days, large caliber, painful. Occasional soiling of underwear.

physical_exam.abdomen: Soft, palpable stool in left lower quadrant, no tenderness, no masses
physical_exam.rectal: Normal tone, hard stool in rectal vault, no fissures

hidden_data.abdominal_xray: Significant fecal loading throughout colon without obstruction

key_learning_points: Rome IV criteria for functional constipation
Behavioral modification: toilet sitting after meals

patient_responses.bowel: He goes once a week, and it's huge and painful
patient_responses.soiling: He sometimes leaks stool in his underwear
patient_responses.diet: He doesn't eat many fruits or vegetables

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Functional constipation with encopresis.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is functional constipation. The history + normal exam + fecal loading confirm it.

abdominal_xray.comment: Shows fecal loading - not always needed.

feedback.attending_note: Functional constipation: Disimpact with PEG 3350 1-1.5g/kg/day for 3-6 days. Maintenance: PEG 0.4-0.8g/kg/day for at least 2 months. Behavioral: toilet sitting 5-10 min after meals. Increase fiber and fluids. Follow up regularly.
